lessons.title: Rooms
lessons.topic: Describing the different parts of a house
lessons.context: Describing the different parts of a house.

steps.instruction: Let's think about houses! Answer these questions.

questions.question_text: Where do you sleep at night?
questions.hint: This room has a bed in it!

questions.question_text: Where does your family eat dinner together?
questions.hint: This is where we cook and eat food!

questions.question_text: Where do you take a bath?
questions.hint: This room has a bathtub or shower!

items.definition: The room where we make food.
A room to wash and use the toilet.
A room where you sleep.
A room to sit and relax.
items.example: We play in the living room.

items.definition: We cook and eat here
We wash and take a bath here
We sleep here at night
We sit and watch TV here

steps.title: Emma's New House
steps.text: Emma has a new house. There is a big kitchen with a yellow table. There are two bedrooms upstairs. Her bedroom has a blue bed. There is a bathroom next to her bedroom. The living room has a soft sofa. Emma loves her new house!
steps.scene_description: Happy young girl showing her bedroom in a new house, bright and colorful children's room

questions.question: What color is the table in the kitchen?
questions.locate_text: There is a big kitchen with a yellow table.

questions.question: Where is the bathroom?
questions.locate_text: There is a bathroom next to her bedroom.

questions.question: How many bedrooms are there in Emma's house?

steps.rule_pattern: There is a ___ / There are ___ / Is there a ___? / Are there any ___?
steps.explanation: We use 'There is' for ONE thing and 'There are' for MORE THAN ONE thing in our house.
examples: There is a kitchen in my house.
There are two bedrooms in my house.

items.sentence: There ___ a kitchen in my house.
There ___ three bedrooms upstairs.
I ___ in the living room.
___ there any windows in the living room?
There ___ a big sofa in the living room.
There are a kitchen in my house.
Is there any bedrooms upstairs?
There is three bathroom in my house.
Are there a living room downstairs?
There am two bedrooms in my house.
There is a bathroom in my house.
items.corrected: There is a bathroom in my house.

items.sentence: There are a kitchen downstairs.
Are there any bedrooms upstairs?
items.explanation: Correct! Use 'Are' to ask about plural bedrooms

items.sentence: Is there two living rooms?
items.explanation: Use 'Are' with two living rooms, not 'Is'

items.sentence: There are three bedrooms in my house.
items.corrected: There are three bedrooms in my house.

items.question: _____ a kitchen in my house.
items.explanation: Use 'There is' to say one kitchen exists

items.question: _____ two bathrooms upstairs?
items.explanation: Use 'Are there' to ask about plural bathrooms

items.question: _____ a living room downstairs?
items.explanation: Use 'Is there' to ask about one living room

items.question: _____ any windows in the bedroom?
items.explanation: Use 'Are there' with 'any' for plural windows

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let me tell you about my house. There is a big kitchen with a red table. There are three bedrooms upstairs. My bedroom has a blue bed. Is there a bathroom upstairs? Yes, there is one bathroom next to my bedroom. The living room is downstairs and it has a soft sofa.

questions.question: How many bedrooms are there in the house?
What color is the bed in the bedroom?
Where is the living room?

steps.prompt: Let's talk about your house! Say these sentences out loud.
steps.expected_text: There is a kitchen in my house.

prompts.prompt: Say: There is a kitchen in my house.
prompts.expected_text: There is a kitchen in my house.

prompts.prompt: Say: There are two bedrooms in my house.
prompts.expected_text: There are two bedrooms in my house.

prompts.prompt: Ask: Is there a bathroom upstairs?

questions.question_text: Which room do you sleep in?
There ___ a kitchen in my house.
___ there any bedrooms upstairs?
True or False: We use 'There are' when talking about more than one room.
Read: 'There are two bathrooms in my house.' How many bathrooms are there?

lessons.title: Furniture
lessons.topic: Finding objects while cleaning a room
lessons.context: Finding objects while cleaning a room.

steps.instruction: Let's think about cleaning! Answer these questions.

questions.question_text: Where do you look when you can't find your toy?
questions.hint: Think about places in your bedroom where toys can hide!

questions.question_text: What furniture do you open to put away your clothes?
questions.hint: It's a big piece of furniture where you keep shirts and pants!

questions.question_text: When Mom says 'Clean your room!', what should you do first?
questions.correct_answer: pick up toys from the floor
options: pick up toys from the floor
questions.hint: Look around! What's lying on the floor that needs to go back to its place?

items.definition: Glass that reflects images.
A tool that tells time.
A tool to talk to people far away.
items.example: I call my friend on the phone.

items.definition: Steps to go up or down.

items.hint: You look at your face in this!
This tells you what time it is!
You can call Mom and Dad with this!
You walk up and down these to go to different floors!

items.sentence: I look in the ___ to see my hair.
The ___ says it's 3 o'clock!
Mom is calling me on the ___.
Be careful when you walk down the ___!
I can see myself in the ___.
We go up the ___ to our bedroom.

steps.title: Clean Up Time!
steps.text: Emma is cleaning her room. She looks under the bed and finds her phone! There is a mirror on the wall. She walks up the stairs with her toys. The clock says it's 4 o'clock. Time to finish!
steps.scene_description: young girl cleaning bedroom, putting toys away, tidy children's room

questions.question: Where does Emma find her phone?
questions.locate_text: She looks under the bed and finds her phone!

questions.question: Is there a mirror in Emma's room?
questions.locate_text: There is a mirror on the wall. She walks up the stairs with her toys.

questions.question: What time does the clock show?

steps.rule_pattern: Is there a ___? / Are there any ___s? / There is a ___ / There are ___s
steps.explanation: We use 'Is there' and 'There is' for ONE thing, and 'Are there' and 'There are' for TWO or MORE things!
examples: Is there a clock in your room? Yes, there is a clock on the wall.
Are there any toys under the bed? Yes, there are three toys under the bed.
There is a mirror in the bathroom. There are stairs in my house.

items.sentence: There ___ stairs in my house.
There ___ a clock on the wall.
Is there ___ mirror?
Are there ___ clocks?
___ there any phones here?
___ there a mirror in the room?
Is there any mirror?
items.explanation: Use 'a' with singular nouns after 'Is there'

items.sentence: There are a phone here.
Are there a clocks?
items.explanation: Use 'any' with plural nouns after 'Are there'

items.sentence: There is two mirror.
Is there stairs?
There am a clock.
Is there a mirror?
items.explanation: Correct! We use 'Is there a' for one thing.

items.sentence: There is stairs.
Are there any phones?
items.explanation: Correct! We use 'Are there any' for more than one thing.

items.sentence: Is there any clocks?
There are toys under the bed.
items.explanation: Correct! 'There are' is used for more than one thing.

items.sentence: There are a phone.

items.question: There ___ two phones here.
items.explanation: Use 'are' with more than one thing

items.question: There ___ a mirror on the wall.
Is there ___ mirror?
items.explanation: Use 'a' with singular nouns after 'Is there'

items.question: Are there ___ toys?
items.explanation: Use 'any' with plural nouns after 'Are there'

items.question: ___ there any stairs?
items.explanation: Use 'Are' for asking about more than one thing

items.question: ___ there a clock in your room?
items.explanation: Use 'Is' for asking about one thing

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let me tell you about my morning. I was cleaning my bedroom and I found my phone under the bed! Then I looked at the clock on the wall. It was 9 o'clock! I walked up the stairs to put my toys away. There is a big mirror in my room and I can see myself when I clean!

questions.question: Where did I find my phone?
What time was it on the clock?
Is there a mirror in my room?
Is the talk about cooking pizza?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Look at your room and answer these questions out loud.
steps.expected_text: There is a mirror in my room.

prompts.prompt: Is there a clock in your room? Say: Yes, there is a clock. OR No, there isn't a clock.
Look around. Tell me: There is a ___ in my room. (Choose: mirror, phone, or clock)
prompts.expected_text: There is a mirror in my room.

prompts.prompt: Ask me a question using 'Are there any...?' (Try: toys, stairs, or phones)
prompts.expected_text: Are there any toys in your room?

questions.question_text: Which one helps you see what time it is?
There ___ a mirror on the wall.
___ there any toys under the bed?
True or False: We use 'Is there' when asking about ONE thing.
Read: 'I walk up the stairs to my room.' Where do I go?

lessons.title: Existence
lessons.topic: Describing what is inside a dream house
lessons.context: Describing what is inside a dream house.

steps.instruction: Let's think about houses! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do you sleep in your house?
questions.hint: This room has a bed in it!

questions.question_text: What room has a table where your family eats?
questions.hint: Think about where you sit down to eat dinner!

questions.question_text: Where do you wash your hands and take a bath?
questions.hint: This room has a toilet and a sink!

items.definition: Words to show one thing exists.
Words to show more than one thing exists.
A lot of things.
A small amount or part of something.

steps.title: My Dream House
steps.text: I have a dream house! There is a big kitchen with a red table. There are many toys in my bedroom. There are some books on the shelf. Is there a garden? Yes, there is!
steps.scene_description: happy child drawing or imagining a colorful house, dreamy atmosphere

questions.question: What color is the table in the kitchen?
questions.locate_text: There is a big kitchen with a red table.

questions.question: Where are the many toys?
questions.locate_text: There are many toys in my bedroom.

questions.question: Is there a garden in the dream house?

steps.rule_pattern: There is ___ (one thing). / There are ___ (two or more things).
steps.explanation: We say 'there is' for one thing and 'there are' for many things in a place.
examples: There is a bed in my bedroom.
There are some chairs in the kitchen.
There are many books on the shelf.

items.sentence: There ___ a table in the kitchen.
There ___ some beds in my house.
___ there a garden?
___ there any toys in the bedroom?
There are ___ books on the shelf.
There are a bed in my room.
Is there any chairs here?
There is many toys in the box.
There are some book on the table.
Are there a kitchen in your house?
There is a garden in my house.
items.corrected: There is a garden in my house.
items.explanation: Correct! 'Is' is used with one garden

items.sentence: There are some chair in the room.
items.corrected: There are some chairs in the room.

items.sentence: Is there any books on the shelf?
items.corrected: Are there any books on the shelf?

items.sentence: There are many toys in my bedroom.
items.corrected: There are many toys in my bedroom.
items.explanation: Correct! 'Are' is used with many toys

items.sentence: There is some windows in the house.
items.corrected: There are some windows in the house.

items.question: There ___ a bed in my dream house.
There ___ many chairs in the kitchen.
___ there a bathroom in your house?
items.explanation: Use 'Is' to ask about one thing

items.question: ___ there any toys in the box?
items.explanation: Use 'Are' to ask about plural things

items.question: There are ___ books on my shelf.
items.explanation: Use 'some' in positive sentences with plural

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let me tell you about my dream house. There is a big kitchen with a yellow table. There are many windows in the living room. Is there a garden? Yes, there is! There are some flowers in the garden.

questions.question: What color is the table in the kitchen?
Where are the many windows?
What is in the garden?

steps.prompt: Tell me about your dream house! Use 'there is' and 'there are'.
steps.expected_text: There is a bed in my bedroom.

prompts.prompt: Say: There is a kitchen in my dream house.
prompts.expected_text: There is a kitchen in my dream house.

prompts.prompt: Say: There are some toys in my bedroom.
prompts.expected_text: There are some toys in my bedroom.

prompts.prompt: Ask your friend: Is there a garden in your dream house?
prompts.expected_text: Is there a garden in your dream house?

questions.question_text: There ___ a bed in my bedroom.
Which word means 'a lot of'?
___ there any chairs in the kitchen?
True or False: We say 'There are' for many things.
Read: 'There are some toys in my room.' How many toys are there?
questions.correct_answer: We don't know the exact number
options: We don't know the exact number

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.topic: Matching furniture to the correct rooms
lessons.context: Matching furniture to the correct rooms.

steps.instruction: Let's think about our homes! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do you sleep at night?
questions.hint: This room has a bed in it!

questions.question_text: Where does mummy cook dinner?
questions.hint: This room has a cooker and fridge!

questions.question_text: What do you sit on when you watch TV?
questions.hint: It's soft and long, and the whole family can sit on it!

items.definition: The room where we cook food
The room where we take a bath
The room where we sleep
You look at yourself in this
This tells us what time it is
We use this to call people
We climb these to go upstairs
A lot of something
The room where we cook food
The room where we take a bath
The room where we sleep
The room where the family watches TV
You look at yourself in this
This tells us what time it is
We use this to call people
We climb these to go upstairs

items.question: Which room has a sofa and TV?
What do you look in to see your face?
We use 'there is' with how many things?
We use 'there are' with how many things?
Where do you have a shower?

steps.title: Emma's New House
steps.text: Emma has a new house. There is a big bed in the bedroom. There are two chairs in the kitchen. Is there a mirror in the bathroom? Yes, there is! Are there any toys in the living room? Yes, there are many toys!
steps.scene_description: happy young girl standing in a bright modern house interior with furniture visible in different rooms

questions.question: Where is the big bed?
questions.locate_text: There is a big bed in the bedroom.

questions.question: Are there chairs in the kitchen?
questions.locate_text: There are two chairs in the kitchen.

questions.question: What is in the living room?
questions.locate_text: Are there any toys in the living room?

steps.explanation: We use 'There is' for ONE thing and 'There are' for MORE THAN ONE thing.
examples: There is a bed in the bedroom.
There are two chairs in the kitchen.
Is there a mirror in the bathroom? Yes, there is!
Are there any toys in the living room? Yes, there are!

items.correctSentence: There is a clock in the kitchen

items.question: ___ a bathroom in the house?
items.explanation: Use 'Is there' to ask about one thing

items.question: ___ any clocks in the bedroom?
items.explanation: Use 'Are there' to ask about many things

items.question: ___ a big kitchen in my house.
items.explanation: Use 'There is' to tell about one thing

items.explanation: Use 'There are' to tell about many things

items.question: ___ a mirror and a clock?
items.explanation: Use 'Are there' to ask about more than one thing

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let me tell you about my house. There is a big mirror in my bathroom. There are three clocks in my living room. Is there a phone in the kitchen? Yes, there is! Are there any stairs? Yes, there are many stairs!
